title: GitHub DevOps & Version Control
subtitle: Git fundamentals, GitHub workflows, and the CI/CD patterns that ship.
summary: Git and GitHub are the backbone of modern software delivery. This course covers what every engineer needs: Git internals you actually need (commits, branches, rebases, conflicts), GitHub workflows that scale (PRs, code review, branch protection), and CI/CD with GitHub Actions (testing, deployment, security). Beyond the basics: production patterns for monorepos, release management, and DevOps practices that hold up at scale.

lessons.title: Commits, the object model, and what Git stores
lessons.objective: Git as a graph of immutable snapshots.

lessons.title: Merge vs rebase — when to use which

modules.title: Working with GitHub
modules.objective: PRs, reviews, and the collaboration model.

lessons.title: The pull request workflow
lessons.objective: Branch, push, PR, merge — done right.

lessons.title: GitHub Issues and Projects
lessons.objective: Tracking work where the code lives.

lessons.title: Common CI pipelines
lessons.objective: Test, lint, type-check on every PR.

modules.title: Release management
modules.objective: Tag, version, and deploy with confidence.

lessons.title: Monorepo strategies
lessons.objective: Multiple packages, one repo, sane builds.
